name: archetypes/process-flow
title: Process flow
description: A 3–5 step pipeline as a horizontal band: numbered markers, terse step titles, one sentence per step, revealed in order.
useCases: explaining how a release, request, or review moves through stages
walking an audience through a workflow one advance at a time

steps.description: 3–5 stages; two is a comparison, six is two slides.

motion.notes: Each ls-flow__step carries ls-reveal with data-step 1..N so the pipeline builds in order; the sequence is the argument.

icons.guidance: The numbered markers are the iconography; do not add sprite icons to steps.

useWhen: the order of stages carries the meaning
each stage can be said in one sentence
avoidWhen: stages need evidence, code, or sub-bullets — give each stage its own slide
the steps are dated milestones rather than a pipeline — use components/list with ls-list--timeline

alternatives.when: only two stages, framed as a contrast

attributes.name: data-ls-archetype
attributes.description: Marks the slide for contract linting and deck scorecards.

usage: Step titles are verbs ("Build", "Canary", "Promote"), not noun phrases.
Keep step bodies parallel in length and grammar; one long step makes the band ragged.
Give the ls-flow a role="img" aria-label summarizing the whole pipeline.
